seoTitle: Mount Tom vs Mount Tully — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Tom and Mount Tully. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Tom vs Mount Tully: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Tom and Mount Tully.
heading: Mount Tom vs Mount Tully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Tom, Queensland and Mount Tully, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Tully has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Mount Tom (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Tom or Mount Tully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Tom has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Mount Tully scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Tom scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Tully scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Tom or Mount Tully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Tully has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
